title: How To Build A Dividend Portfolio With $50,000 Among 20 Dividend Paying Companies/ETFs
text: The construction of a dividend-income portfolio brings several benefits for investors, one of which is generating extra income. In today's article, I will show you how you could allocate the amount of $50,000 among 20 dividend paying companies/ETFs that I consider to be attractive right now. Due to its allocation, the portfolio reaches a Weighted Average Dividend Yield [TTM] of 3.86%, indicating that its dividend payments could help you cover your monthly expenses.

title: Philip Morris International Submits Applications to Commercialize IQOS ILUMA Tobacco Heating System to U.S. Food and Drug Administration
text: STAMFORD, CT--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Philip Morris International Inc. (PMI) (NYSE: PM) on October 20 submitted Premarket Tobacco Product Applications (PMTAs) and Modified Risk Tobacco Product Applications (MRTPAs) for IQOS ILUMA heated tobacco products with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). A PMTA marketing order is required to commercialize any new tobacco product in the United States. text: Philip Morris beat quarterly earnings estimates and missed revenue expectations, but sales of heated tobacco and oral nicotine products were strong.

title: Philip Morris (PM) Q3 Earnings Beat Estimates
text: Philip Morris (PM) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.67 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.61 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.53 per share a year ago.

title: Philip Morris earnings top forecasts but revenue falls short
text: Philip Morris International Inc (NYSE:PM) called its third quarter performance “very strong” as quarterly revenue topped the $9 billion mark for the first time. The Marlboro and Benson & Hedges cigarette maker reported operating income of $3.37 billion, up 19% from $2.97 billion the year prior.

title: Philip Morris Stock Tips Lower After Revenue Miss
text: The tobacco company still posted an earnings beat. It said third-quarter EPS was $1.67.

title: Philip Morris International adjusted profit beats estimate
text: Philip Morris International Inc. PM, -0.19% on Thursday said its third-quarter net income rose to $3.37 billion, or $1.32 a share, from $2.97 billion, or $1.34 a share, in the year-ago quarter. The tobacco company's adjusted third quarter profit of $1.67 a share beat the FactSet consensus estimate of $1.62 a share.

text: Philip Morris International Inc (NYSE:PM) is slated to report earnings before the market opens on Thursday, when investors will get another chance to evaluate the company's smoke-free product business.  The Marlboro parent is expected to post earnings of $1.61 per share on revenue of $9.17 billion, compared to $1.34 per share on revenue of $8.03 billion a year earlier.

title: Philip Morris: How To Change A Failing Industry
text: Philip Morris is the best investment option for exposure to the tobacco industry's pivot towards healthier alternatives. Smoke-free products are gaining traction and are expected to represent a significant portion of industry volumes by 2030. Nicotine, the key ingredient in these alternatives, has positive effects on health and does not have harmful effects like cigarettes.

title: Philip Morris International to Host Webcast of 2023 Third-Quarter and September Year-to-Date Results

title: 5 Ideal Fortune 500 Top October Dividend Dogs

title: Philip Morris International Joins We Card Retailer Support Program
text: STAMFORD, CT--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Philip Morris International Inc. (PMI) (NYSE: PM) today announced that it has joined The We Card Program, Inc., a national nonprofit serving the nation's retailers of age-restricted products. The company's Swedish Match affiliate will serve on We Card's Manufacturer Advisory Council. title: Dividend Growth Predictions For 15 Companies Announcing Annual Boosts In October
text: Fifteen long-term dividend growth companies, AbbVie among them, will announce their annual increases in October. Most of the increases will be in the single digit range, with 10%+ increases expected from A. O. Smith, Lincoln Electric and Waste Connections. Microsoft announced a 10% dividend increase in September, and we're still waiting for announcements from McDonald's and Lockheed Martin.
